description:
You are acting as an “artifact-manager” who curates in-house binary repositories.  
Your goal is to create a **versioned package manifest** for the repository that lives in  

  /home/user/repos/stable

and then expose a convenient “latest” pointer to that manifest.

Current repository state (already present before you start):
• Directory: /home/user/repos/stable  
• Two Debian package files (empty placeholder binaries)
  1. /home/user/repos/stable/libbar_2.5.1_amd64.deb
  2. /home/user/repos/stable/libfoo_1.0.0_amd64.deb

Required final state
1. A new directory /home/user/manifests must exist (create it if needed).
2. Inside that directory create a **single, newly generated file** named  
   /home/user/manifests/stable_manifest_20240101.csv
3. The .csv file must have **exactly three comma-separated columns** in this order:
      filename,sha256,size_bytes
   • The very first line is a header containing those three literal column names.  
   • One subsequent line per “.deb” file found directly under /home/user/repos/stable.  
   • Lines must be sorted in strictly lexicographic order by filename.  
   • The sha256 column must contain the full 64-character lowercase hexadecimal digest.  
   • size_bytes must be the decimal size of the file in bytes.  
   • Terminate every line (including the last) with a single UNIX newline (LF).  
   Example layout (values will differ):
     filename,sha256,size_bytes
     libbar_2.5.1_amd64.deb,<hash>,0
     libfoo_1.0.0_amd64.deb,<hash>,0
4. After writing the file, create (or replace) a symbolic link  
   /home/user/manifests/stable_latest.csv
   that points to stable_manifest_20240101.csv (use a **relative** link target, i.e. “stable_manifest_20240101.csv”).
5. Finally, display (print to STDOUT) the full contents of your new
   /home/user/manifests/stable_manifest_20240101.csv so that a human reviewer can see the result.

Hints & constraints
• You do NOT need root privileges.  
• A typical solution will need no more than 5-10 shell commands (directory creation, sha256sum, sort, redirection, ln -s, etc.).  
• Do not hard-code the hash values—compute them from the files.  
• If you re-run your solution it should overwrite/refresh the manifest and the symlink without errors.

The automated checker will:
• Verify that /home/user/manifests/stable_manifest_20240101.csv exists.  
• Confirm its header and line formatting exactly.  
• Confirm that each listed file really exists in /home/user/repos/stable.  
• Verify the sha256 digests and sizes.  
• Verify that /home/user/manifests/stable_latest.csv is a symlink whose target is “stable_manifest_20240101.csv”.  
• Inspect your STDOUT to ensure it shows the manifest content verbatim.

Work entirely in the terminal to accomplish these steps.
truth:
Pre-existing repository files (all world-readable, empty placeholders):
1. /home/user/repos/stable/libbar_2.5.1_amd64.deb  
   • size: 0 bytes  
   • sha256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
2. /home/user/repos/stable/libfoo_1.0.0_amd64.deb  
   • size: 0 bytes  
   • sha256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

Required artefacts after correct completion:
A. File: /home/user/manifests/stable_manifest_20240101.csv  
   Exact byte-for-byte content (include trailing LF):
filename,sha256,size_bytes
libbar_2.5.1_amd64.deb,e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855,0
libfoo_1.0.0_amd64.deb,e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855,0

B. Symlink: /home/user/manifests/stable_latest.csv  
   • Must exist.  
   • Readlink should output exactly: stable_manifest_20240101.csv

Permissions:
• /home/user/manifests: 755 (drwxr-xr-x)  
• Manifest file: 644 (-rw-r--r--)  
• Symlink: 777 lrwxrwxrwx (default for ln -s).

The checker will open the manifest, recompute the sha256 of each “.deb” file, compare sizes, ensure line order, header, newline termination, then verify the symlink target.  
Printing the manifest to STDOUT is also asserted by capturing program output.
name: task_000000_3822c1b3
